Array programming with NumPy

Smith, Robert Kern, Matti Picus, Stephan Hoyer, Marten H. van Kerkwijk, Matthew Brett, Allan Haldane, Jaime Fernández del Río, Mark Wiebe, Pearu Peterson, Pierre Gérard-Marchant, Kevin Sheppard, Tyler Reddy, Warren Weckesser, Hameer Abbasi, Christoph Gohlke, Travis E. Overview Story Impact Roles Access Mentions
More
Teaching Who's Citing

The Story So Far

Short impact summary

The article "Array programming with NumPy," published in Springer Nature in 2020, has garnered significant scholarly attention, amassing over 22,000 citations. This high citation count indicates its substantial influence within the fields of computer science and computational science, particularly in relation to Python programming. The work has also made its way into educational contexts, being mentioned in at least one open courseware (OCW) and a YouTube video.

Full narrative

"Array programming with NumPy" represents a critical contribution to the scientific community, specifically in its facilitation of array-oriented computing in Python. The prominence of this article is evidenced not only by its citation metrics, which stand at 22,112, but also by its widespread adoption across numerous high-visibility journals, including but not limited to The Astrophysical Journal and Springer Nature Communications. These metrics clearly underscore the article's role as a significant reference point within its field.

Moreover, the article serves as a pedagogical anchor, reflecting its integration into educational resources such as universities and online learning platforms. It has been included in course materials and discussions, further solidifying its importance in training new generations of scientists and programmers. The presence of educational references highlights the article's capacity to influence both theoretical understanding and practical skill development in the programming community.

On social platforms, there has been ongoing community discourse, with mentions noted on Wikipedia and other forums, contributing to the broader conversation around Python and scientific computing. This active discussion is a testament to the article's relevance and the continuous evolution of knowledge in this area.

In terms of reuse, the article has been cited in a diverse array of works, confirming its extensive impact across multiple disciplines, including physics, biology, and artificial intelligence. This breadth of applicability illustrates the versatility of array programming concepts as introduced in the article, underpinning their relevance to both academic research and practical applications across various sectors.
